intent_detection.developer_intent: retaste an existing product kernel from fresh ICP research
intent_detection.product_intent: make AI coding work inspectable, evidence-backed, and customer-aligned

routing.reason: existing taste files require defineicp proposal-first semantics

deepresearch_plan.stop_condition: ICP and taste proposal are traceable to strong claims

cited.claim_supported: research synthesis should expose gaps and avoid assumptions

reviewed_but_not_cited.why_downweighted: less authoritative than repo evidence and UX sources

claim_ledger.claim: The ICP values verified agent work more than raw speed.
claim_ledger.label: repo-derived
claim_ledger.evidence: taste.vision and README emphasize right results and evidence.
claim_ledger.taste_impact: Keep verification and rollback as kernel invariants.

claim_ledger.claim: The product should be easier for first-run operators to understand.
claim_ledger.label: current-source-backed
claim_ledger.evidence: current UX source supports gap discovery and communication artifacts.

parallel_decision.why_not_more: five lenses cover the useful uncertainty without slot filling

primary.segment: technical owner-operators using AI agents on production-bound repos
primary.buyer_user_distinction: the buyer is usually the same founder or CTO who operates the harness daily
primary.job_to_be_done: turn ambiguous work into researched, spec-backed, implemented, verified changes
pain_points: parallel work is hard to trust
primary.buyer_context: technical founder or CTO with tooling authority
primary.adoption_channel: Claude Code slash skill and one-command setup
disqualifiers: users who only want unchecked code generation

anti_icp: teams optimizing only for shortest prompt-to-code path

taste_md_changes.change: Add first-run operator confidence as a product taste goal.

taste_vision_changes.change: Name technical owner-operators as the primary ICP.

likely_mistakes: treating inferred buying context as proven
